description_long: This object is a large, cylindrical bomb with a rounded nose and a slightly tapered tail section. The surface is predominantly a matte greenish-blue, with some areas appearing darker, possibly indicating reinforced or metallic sections. There are two prominent stabilizing fins at the rear, and a recessed area on the top that may house a fuse or arming mechanism. The body shows some surface wear and minor pitting, suggesting exposure to the elements or rough handling. No visible markings or warning labels are present, and the overall construction appears robust and industrial, typical of aerial ordnance.
description: A large, cylindrical bomb with a rounded nose, stabilizing fins, and a matte greenish-blue surface, showing signs of wear and robust construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, recessed top section, and partial visibility of the stabilizing fins.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical body, full length, and the two stabilizing fins at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear-angled view focusing on the tail section and the open or darkened rear end of the bomb.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the length of the bomb, the stabilizing fins, and the recessed top area.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The recessed area on the top likely houses a mechanical or electrical fuse, which could be triggered by impact or a timed mechanism.
